user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 17:55 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with executive brief. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 13:02 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with executive brief. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 09:38 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:06:00] David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:10:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:12:00] David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:17:00] David (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.
